name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax Title Loans is located at 105 S Ron McNair Blvd, Lake City, SC 29560. Google reviewers rate the branch 5.0 stars across 872 reviews. Call +1 843-394-2136.
description_long:
TitleMax Title Loans was founded on September 1, 1998, with its first location in Columbus, Georgia. Today it is headquartered at 15 Bull Street, Suite 200, Savannah, Georgia, and operates as a subsidiary of TMX Finance LLC — a Delaware holding company owned by Tracy Young of Savannah. With over 1,100 physical storefronts spanning 16 states, TitleMax is one of the largest title lending operations in the United States. The company holds no CDFI certification, HUD approval, or NFCC membership, as none of those frameworks apply to its business model.

TitleMax's core product is the vehicle title loan: a short-term, secured loan in which the borrower pledges their car or motorcycle title as collateral and continues driving the vehicle during repayment. Loan amounts reach up to approximately $10,000 depending on the vehicle's value and state regulations. In Georgia, the same product is legally structured as a title pawn. TitleMax also offers unsecured personal loans up to $2,500 in select states, and an online personal line of credit available exclusively in Idaho. No traditional credit check is required to apply, and the company does not require a bank account to qualify. Funds can be disbursed in as little as 30 minutes at a physical store.

What distinguishes TitleMax operationally is its sheer physical footprint — over 1,100 locations provide walk-in access that online-only lenders cannot match. Borrowers retain use of their vehicle throughout the loan term, and the application process is designed for speed. The company offers a mobile app on both iOS and Android as well as an online portal at account.titlemax.com, where customers can check their balance, review their payment schedule, view payoff amounts, and access payment history. Text message payment reminders are also included as a standard feature.

TitleMax fills a real gap for consumers who are locked out of conventional credit markets — people with damaged credit histories, no bank account, or urgent cash needs. However, the cost of that access is steep: reported APRs run from roughly 100% to over 300%, placing these products among the most expensive forms of consumer borrowing available. The company is not BBB accredited, and BBB profiles across multiple locations show substantial complaint volumes related to undisclosed fees, payment disputes, and vehicle repossession. Borrowers who miss payments face the loss of their primary vehicle, making TitleMax loans a high-stakes financial decision that should be considered only after exhausting lower-cost alternatives.
address: 105 S Ron McNair Blvd, Lake City, SC 29560

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee policy identified for any TitleMax lending product.

pros: One of the largest title lenders in the U.S. with 1,100+ physical store locations across 16 states
No traditional credit check required — accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit history
Funds disbursed in as little as 30 minutes at a store location
Borrowers keep and drive their vehicle throughout the entire repayment period
No bank account required to qualify for a loan
Mobile app (iOS and Android) plus online portal for balance, payoff amount, and payment history
Founded in 1998 — over 27 years of title lending operations with established processes
cons: APRs reported at 100%–300%+, making these among the most expensive loan products available to consumers
Failure to repay risks repossession of the borrower's vehicle — potentially their only transportation
Not BBB accredited; numerous complaints on file across locations covering undisclosed fees, repossession disputes, and payment issues
Only operates in 16 states — unavailable to the majority of U.S. consumers
Maximum loan amount of ~$10,000 is tied directly to vehicle value, not borrower income or need
best_for: Consumers with a lien-free or nearly paid-off vehicle who need fast cash and cannot qualify for traditional credit
Individuals with poor or no credit history who have been denied by banks or credit unions
Borrowers without a bank account who need same-day funding in an emergency
Residents of one of the 16 states where TitleMax operates who need a short-term secured loan
diagnosis: TitleMax is genuinely best for consumers who own a paid-off vehicle, need cash urgently, and have no viable lower-cost borrowing options due to credit history or lack of a bank account. The critical caveat is the triple-digit APR — borrowers who cannot repay quickly face compounding costs and the real possibility of losing the vehicle used as collateral.
services: Car title loans (up to approximately $10,000, secured by vehicle title)
Motorcycle title loans (same collateral structure as car title loans)
Title pawns (Georgia locations only — legally distinct from a loan under Georgia law)
Unsecured personal loans (up to $2,500, available in select states)
Online personal line of credit (Idaho only, online product)
Same-day funding (as little as 30 minutes at physical store)
Online account portal — balance, payment schedule, payoff amount, payment history
Mobile app (iOS and Android) for account management
In-person customer service at 1,100+ store locations
